description_long: This house features a traditional thatched roof with thick, layered straw, giving it a rustic and rural appearance. The front facade displays a log construction with visible horizontal beams, a central wooden door, and small, symmetrically placed windows. A covered porch runs along the front, supported by wooden posts, and includes a bench and a small entry area. The side views reveal the steep pitch of the roof and the compact, rectangular footprint of the structure. The rear of the house is simpler, with fewer windows and a single door, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The overall look suggests a historical or vernacular rural dwelling, likely from Eastern Europe or a similar region.
description: A rustic, single-story house with a thick thatched roof, log walls, and a covered front porch supported by wooden posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two small windows, a covered porch with wooden posts, and a bench.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steeply pitched thatched roof and a small, enclosed side area with a wooden door.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler facade, featuring a single door and minimal windows, emphasizing the log construction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the thatched roof and the plain log wall with no visible openings.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with a covered porch, supported by wooden posts and featuring a bench.
